name: Ethanol, 200 proof (absolute)
striking_fact: Vapor pressure is high enough to cause volume drift on every aspirate — pre-wet tips 3× before dispensing.

hazard_statements: H225: Highly flammable liquid and vapour

sds_facts.storage: Store at 4°C, tightly sealed, away from ignition sources. In a flammables cabinet.

bench_knowledge: Pre-wet tip 3× before aspirating: aspirate and expel ethanol without dispensing into sample. This saturates the headspace inside the tip and stops volume drift.
Ethanol evaporates from the tip exterior within seconds; work quickly and keep the reservoir sealed between uses.
Absolute (200 proof) ethanol is hygroscopic — opened bottles absorb atmospheric water. Use a desiccant closure or aliquot into sealed amber vials.
Never use polystyrene reservoirs; use polypropylene. Ethanol is compatible with both, but PS reservoirs may leach styrene oligomers with extended contact.
Bleach + ethanol generates chloroform and chloroacetone — do NOT decontaminate ethanol spills with bleach.
At −20°C ethanol is fully liquid (mp −114°C) and stays fluid; used deliberately for cold methanol/ethanol fixation of cells.
